input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10. This is a known change in .NET MAUI / Android behavior.

Let me provide a concise, helpful answer based on my knowledge of .NET MAUI changes.

In .NET 10 (and actually starting with .NET 9 in some previews), the Android behavior around `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` changed because Google deprecated `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` in Android API 30+. 

.NET MAUI moved to edge-to-edge rendering in .NET 10, which changed how the keyboard interacts with the layout. The recommended solutions are using the built-in `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` behavior to automatically scroll content when the keyboard appears, or manually handling Android's `WindowInsets` API to adjust the layout when the soft input appears.

For a chat screen with a pinned input bar, the practical approach is to use a Grid layout with the input anchored at the bottom and the message content in a ScrollView above it, enabling the auto-scroll behavior to keep the conversation visible as the keyboard slides up.

On Android specifically, since .NET 10 defaults to edge-to-edge rendering, I need to respond to the IME inset directly rather than relying on the deprecated resize mode—adjusting the layout's padding based on keyboard visibility is the modern solution.

For MAUI, `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` should handle most of the scrolling automatically, but for a chat interface where the entire input bar needs to move up with the keyboard, I'll likely need platform-specific code to listen to the IME insets and reposition the UI accordingly.
